steps.title: Set up the intersection equation
steps.content:
The graphs intersect where $x-c=-5(x-10)^2$. Expanding and moving everything to one side gives:
$$5x^2-99x+(500-c)=0$$

steps.title: Find the tangent boundary first
steps.content: Two distinct real solutions require the discriminant to be positive: $$99^2-4(5)(500-c)\gt 0.$$ Simplify: $$9801-10000+20c\gt 0$$ $$20c-199\gt 0$$ $$c\gt \frac{199}{20}\approx9.95.$$

steps.title: Pick the listed value that gives two intersections
steps.content: $c=\frac{199}{20}$ gives one repeated solution, not two distinct solutions. The value must be greater than that boundary. Among the choices, only $20$ is greater, so D is correct.

choiceElimination:
• '6': $6 \lt 199/20 \approx 9.95$, so the discriminant is negative and there are no real solutions. ✗
  • '9': $9 \lt 9.95$, so the discriminant is still negative. ✗
  • '$\frac{199}{20}$': Exactly at the threshold, the discriminant is $0$, giving one repeated solution instead of two distinct solutions. ✗
  • '20': $20 \gt 9.95$, so the discriminant is positive and there are two distinct real solutions. ✓
That matches the correct answer - D is correct.
